meaning: adj.通常的,习惯的,按照风俗习惯的
examples: Some are so accustomed to pigging out, they can't cut back.
Mary Jo is still accustomed to travelling everywhere in style.
She was a person accustomed to having eight hours' sleep a night.
That just might deliver the kind of double-digit earnings growth that a consumer brands executive is accustomed to.
Mity-Lite (recently renamed Mity Enterprises) was accustomed to delivering consistent 20% annual earnings growth.
